title: Tender Shuttle: Single Tender Sustained Flow
question: Engine 181 is operating at a rural structure fire with no hydrants available. Engine 181 is drafting from a portable tank. Tender 184 has a 2,500-gallon tank. Use 90% usable tank capacity. Fill time is 4 minutes, travel to the scene is 6 minutes, dump time is 3 minutes, and return travel is 6 minutes. What sustained water supply in GPM can this single-tender shuttle provide?
formulaBreakdown: This is a water-supply scenario. The final answer is sustained shuttle flow in GPM, not pump pressure.
Tender Shuttle GPM = usable water delivered per trip ÷ total shuttle cycle time in minutes.
Cycle time = fill time + travel to scene + dump time + return travel.
Pump pressure fields are 0 because this scenario is not asking for pump discharge pressure.
instructorExplanation: This problem estimates sustained water supply from a single tender shuttle by dividing usable gallons per trip by the full round-trip cycle time.
explainMistake: Common mistakes: using full tank capacity instead of usable water, forgetting fill time, forgetting dump time, only counting one-way travel, or calculating gallons per trip but not dividing by cycle time.

variations.change: Change tender capacity from 2,500 gallons to 3,000 gallons. Same 90% usable capacity and 19-minute cycle.
Change travel time to scene from 6 minutes to 8 minutes. Same 2,500-gallon tender and 90% usable capacity.
Change dump time from 3 minutes to 5 minutes. Same 2,500-gallon tender and 90% usable capacity.
